title: 形容詞の語法
fullTitle: 第20章　形容詞の語法

problems.question: Driving downtown to the stadium for the baseball game was easy. We got there quickly because there was (　　) traffic.
problems.ja: 野球の試合のために車で繁華街を通って野球場へ行くのはたやすかった。交通量がほとんどなかったので，すぐにそこに着いた。
problems.en: Driving downtown to the stadium for the baseball game was easy. We got there quickly because there was little traffic.

problems.question: I can't decide now. I need (　　) time to think about it.
problems.ja: 今は決められません。それについて考える時間が少し必要です。
problems.en: I can't decide now. I need a little time to think about it.

problems.question: My daughter has (　　) close friends at school.
problems.ja: 私の娘は学校に数少ない親友がいる。
problems.en: My daughter has a few close friends at school.

problems.question: There were (　　) people at the meeting, so the room was nearly empty.
problems.ja: 会議にはほとんど人がいなかったので，部屋はからっぽに近かった。
problems.en: There were few people at the meeting, so the room was nearly empty.

problems.question: It is not good for your health to put (　　) sugar in your coffee.
problems.ja: コーヒーに砂糖を入れすぎると，健康に良くないですよ。
problems.en: It is not good for your health to put too much sugar in your coffee.

problems.question: Have you had (　　) experiences abroad?
problems.ja: 海外経験は多いですか。
problems.en: Have you had many experiences abroad?

problems.question: There were <u>quite a few</u> students absent from class.
problems.ja: 授業を休んだ学生がかなり大勢いた。
problems.en: There were quite a few students absent from class.

problems.question: We'll get (　　) information from the tourist office.
problems.ja: 私たちは旅行案内所から情報を入手するつもりだ。
problems.en: We'll get some information from the tourist office.

problems.question: Come here and sit down by me. There is (　　) room for you.
problems.ja: ここに来て私のそばに座りなさい。十分なスペースがあるから。
problems.en: Come here and sit down by me. There is enough room for you.

problems.question: A great (　　) of money has been spent on this building.
problems.ja: この建物にたくさんのお金が費やされてきた。
problems.en: A great deal of money has been spent on this building.

problems.question: <u>A great number of</u> members opposed my proposal at the meeting.
problems.ja: 会議では多くの参加者が私の提案に反対した。
problems.en: A great number of members opposed my proposal at the meeting.

problems.question: I don't mind being transferred as long as the job is (　　).
problems.ja: 仕事がおもしろいのであれば，転勤も気にしません。
problems.en: I don't mind being transferred as long as the job is interesting.

problems.question: When he was ①<u>asked</u> why he was studying ②<u>so</u> hard, he replied ③<u>that</u> he was ④<u>interesting</u> in English culture.
problems.ja: なぜそんなに必死に勉強しているのかと聞かれて，彼は，英国文化に興味があるからと答えた。
problems.en: When he was asked why he was studying so hard, he replied that he was interested in English culture.

problems.question: It was (　　) that so many people came to see our performance.
problems.ja: 私たちの公演をそんなにたくさんの人が見に来てくれたのは，胸躍る出来事だった。
problems.en: It was exciting that so many people came to see our performance.

problems.question: When some (　　) children got onto the train, it became very noisy.
problems.ja: 興奮した子どもたちが電車に乗り込んできて，とても騒がしくなった。
problems.en: When some excited children got onto the train, it became very noisy.

problems.question: My job at the company was very (　　).
problems.ja: その会社での私の仕事はとても退屈なものだった。
problems.en: My job at the company was very boring.

problems.question: ①<u>Students were boring</u> ②<u>because</u> the teacher ③<u>just forced them</u> ④<u>to learn grammar</u> and vocabulary.
problems.ja: 先生が文法と語いを学ぶことばかり強要するので，生徒たちはうんざりしていた。
problems.en: Students were bored because the teacher just forced them to learn grammar and vocabulary.

problems.question: They were (　　) with the results.
problems.ja: 彼らはその結果にがっかりした。
problems.en: They were disappointed with the results.

problems.question: "It is ①<u>extremely disappointed</u> to find that so many shops or shopkeepers ②<u>are still</u> prepared to sell knives to ③<u>people under 16</u>," ④<u>said a researcher</u>.
problems.ja: 「16歳未満の人に平気でナイフを売る店や店主が依然として非常に多いということは極めて残念である」と，調査員は述べた。
problems.en: "It is extremely disappointing to find that so many shops or shopkeepers are still prepared to sell knives to people under 16," said a researcher.

problems.question: Many students seem more (　　) now about how to write a research paper.
problems.ja: 研究論文をどう書けばよいのか，今では多くの生徒たちがいっそう困惑しているようだ。
problems.en: Many students seem more confused now about how to write a research paper.

problems.question: George looked (　　) when I apologized to him for my rudeness.
problems.ja: 私の無礼を謝ると，ジョージは満足したようだった。
problems.en: George looked satisfied when I apologized to him for my rudeness.

problems.question: Despite their efforts to capture the bear (　　), they finally had to shoot it.
problems.ja: 彼らはその熊を生け捕りにしようと努力したが，最終的には射殺しなければならなかった。
problems.en: Despite their efforts to capture the bear alive, they finally had to shoot it.

problems.question: I was so tired that it was really hard to stay (　　) in class.
problems.ja: 私は非常に疲れていたので，授業中に起きているのはとても難しかった。
problems.en: I was so tired that it was really hard to stay awake in class.

problems.question:
空所に共通する１語を書きなさい。
(a) We do not have any more information at the (　　) time.
(b) There were 200 people (　　) at the meeting.
problems.ja:
(a) 現在のところ，私たちにはこれ以上の情報はない。
(b) その会議には200人が出席した。
problems.en: There were 200 people present at the meeting.

problems.question: I would like to see you tomorrow. What time (　　)?
problems.ja: 明日あなたにお目にかかりたいと思います。何時が都合がよろしいですか。
problems.en: I would like to see you tomorrow. What time is convenient for you?

problems.question: It seemed (　　) for us to finish the task by the next day.
problems.ja: 私たちが次の日までにその仕事を終えるのは不可能なようだった。
problems.en: It seemed impossible for us to finish the task by the next day.

problems.question: John is not (　　) to come to the class.
problems.ja: ジョンは授業には出られません。
problems.en: John is not able to come to the class.

problems.question: She is (　　) of great patience when she deals with children.
problems.ja: 子どもを扱うにあたって，彼女は多大な忍耐力を発揮できる。
problems.en: She is capable of great patience when she deals with children.

problems.question: The price of fresh vegetables is getting too (　　) to afford them.
problems.ja: 新鮮な野菜の値段が上がりすぎていて，買うことができない。
problems.en: The price of fresh vegetables is getting too high to afford them.

problems.question: The soccer game was shown on a big screen in front of (　　) audience.
problems.ja: そのサッカーの試合は，大観衆の前の巨大スクリーンに映し出された。
problems.en: The soccer game was shown on a big screen in front of a large audience.

problems.question: David and James are (　　) in many ways.
problems.ja: デイビッドとジェームズは多くの点で似ている。
problems.en: David and James are alike in many ways.

problems.question: John's family always enjoyed having (　　) discussions over dinner.
problems.ja: ジョンの家族はいつも，夕食をとりながらの活発な議論を楽しんだ。
problems.en: John's family always enjoyed having lively discussions over dinner.

problems.question: There seems to be no (　　) thing on this deserted island.
problems.ja: この無人島にはまったく生き物がいないようだ。
problems.en: There seems to be no living thing on this deserted island.

problems.question: I've come to think that buying ①<u>in bulk</u> is more ②<u>economic</u> than shopping ③<u>for</u> small ④<u>quantities</u>.
problems.ja: 少量での買い物よりも，大量購入のほうがより経済的だと私は考えるようになった。
problems.en: I've come to think that buying in bulk is more economical than shopping for small quantities.

problems.question: The ①<u>twentieth</u> century ②<u>saw</u> the rapid ③<u>industrious</u> and ④<u>agricultural</u> development of the U.S.
problems.ja: 20世紀，アメリカでは産業と農業の急速な発達が見られた。
problems.en: The twentieth century saw the rapid industrial and agricultural development of the U.S.

problems.question: We would be glad to send (　　) information if you agree to the terms of the contract.
problems.ja: 契約条件に同意していただければ，より詳しい情報をお送りいたします。
problems.en: We would be glad to send further information if you agree to the terms of the contract.

problems.question: We are now in the (　　) half of our training camp.
problems.ja: 今はトレーニング合宿の後半だ。
problems.en: We are now in the latter half of our training camp.

problems.question: Have you seen his (　　) movie?
problems.ja: あなたは彼が監督した映画の最新作を見ましたか。
problems.en: Have you seen his latest movie?

problems.question: Every morning he spends <u>considerable</u> time reading all the pages of three newspapers.
problems.ja: 毎朝彼は，新聞３紙のすべてのページを読むことにかなりの時間を費やす。
problems.en: Every morning he spends considerable time reading all the pages of three newspapers.

problems.question: Because of (　　) weather conditions, Shizuoka Prefecture has an advantage in the production of fruits and vegetables.
problems.ja: 天候条件がよいため，静岡県は果物や野菜の生産において有利である。
problems.en: Because of favorable weather conditions, Shizuoka Prefecture has an advantage in the production of fruits and vegetables.

problems.question: Our charity project was (　　) because John gave us generous support.
problems.ja: ジョンの惜しみない支援のおかげで，私たちの慈善事業は成功を収めた。
problems.en: Our charity project was successful because John gave us generous support.

problems.question: King Arthur of England was an (　　) person.
problems.ja: イギリスのアーサー王は架空の人物だった。
problems.en: King Arthur of England was an imaginary person.

problems.question: I sometimes feel I have become less (　　) as I grow older.
problems.ja: 私は年を取るにつれて想像力豊かでなくなってきたと，ときどき感じることがある。
problems.en: I sometimes feel I have become less imaginative as I grow older.

problems.question: We always try to be (　　) of each other's opinions, no matter how much we disagree.
problems.ja: たとえどんなに意見が食い違っても，私たちは互いの意見を尊重するよういつも努めている。
problems.en: We always try to be respectful of each other's opinions, no matter how much we disagree.

problems.question: You should be more (　　) to her feelings.
problems.ja: 君は彼女の気持ちにもう少し敏感になるべきだ。
problems.en: You should be more sensitive to her feelings.

problems.question: The Egyptian vessel is (　　) of art, so it is displayed behind a thick pane of glass in the museum.
problems.ja: そのエジプトのつぼはきわめて貴重な芸術作品なので，博物館内の分厚いガラス板の向こう側に展示されている。
problems.en: The Egyptian vessel is an invaluable work of art, so it is displayed behind a thick pane of glass in the museum.

problems.question: I like my job, but I'd like to do (　　).
problems.ja: 私は自分の仕事が気に入っているが，もっと創造的なことをやってみたい。
problems.en: I like my job, but I'd like to do something more creative.

problems.question: I am ①<u>frightfully</u> busy ②<u>because</u> I have just ③<u>gotten</u> back from a ④<u>two-weeks</u> vacation.
problems.ja: ２週間の休暇から戻ったばかりなので，私は死ぬほど忙しい。
problems.en: I am frightfully busy because I have just gotten back from a two-week vacation.

problems.question: The prize-winner was a (　　) boy.
problems.ja: 受賞者は６歳の少年だった。
problems.en: The prize-winner was a six-year-old boy.

problems.question: The ①<u>suspect</u> is a ②<u>six-feet-tall</u> male, ③<u>wearing</u> a black sweater ④<u>with</u> a white logo.
problems.ja: その容疑者は身長６フィートの男性で，白いロゴ入りの黒いセーターを着ている。
problems.en: The suspect is a six-foot-tall male, wearing a black sweater with a white logo.
